lens: DECIDABILITY AND EXPRESSIVENESS — honest rule recount at kernel granularity; hunt for smuggled flow analysis in joins, region escape, and signature derivation; week-one systems idioms checked as concrete programs against the draft's own rules

findings.title: Callee bodies are checked loan-blind: signature-derived helpers accept dangling tokens
findings.detail: Every statement is rule-legal. Caller side: the statement derives a consume of e (LOAN-6(a)), so the LOAN-4 carve-out permits minting &t under the uniq loan — the exact shape the draft itself blesses in C12/C13. Callee side: LOAN-6(c) checks the body 'with parameters as local bindings' but no rule seeds a loan for the own-token parameter on the co-passed issuer reference, so inside probe loans(t)={} while e carries interior access into t — the soundness sketch's invariant I is violated in the callee frame, and t.range/e.fill compose into a rehash under a live cursor. Variants: fn relocate(t:&uniq 'r table@b, e: own Entry['r,b]) -> Entry['r,b] { let e2 = t.entry(k2); return e2 } typechecks (loans(t)={} in the body, LOAN-2 and LOAN-8 pass) and manufactures two live uniq entry tokens — C3's forbidden state through the front door; the draft's own C13 relock body can only be written as m.lock() under the live guard (double lock). LOAN-4 consume is also undefined for a holder with no recorded issuer (all own confined params). Fix: add LOAN-6(c'): before checking a body, for each own parameter h of loan-holding confined type K['r,b], add (kind of h's loan, h) to loans(x) for every reference parameter x whose form type instantiates ('r,b); define consume of an issuer-less holder as holder death only. If a defender instead claims the LOAN-4 carve-out covers only form-declared ops, then C13's same-instance relock — the draft's own 'legitimate reissue shape' — is uncallable: the draft is either unsound or self-contradictory on this point.

findings.title: Binding-keyed loans and brands: forms held in struct fields either defeat P5's frozen verdict or are silently unusable
findings.detail: LOAN-1 keys loan sets to bindings and LOAN-7 mints brands per owning binding, but the kernel's exclusivity (OWN-5) is per resolved place, and field-projected receivers must exist in the base kernel (P1-style push on a struct field is how any bundled container is used). On the literal LOAN-7 text, both ring values are owned by binding two, share one brand, and unify at feed's single brand parameter — the frozen P5 REJECT is bypassed by one level of struct nesting, and LOAN-2 has no defined loan-set slot for the place two.a either (charitably attaching to loans(two) both over-freezes the sibling field and keeps the brand collision). On the defensive reading — no brand or loan slot exists for places, so issuing ops on fields are rejected fail-closed — no struct-held container can ever produce an entry, cursor, or guard: the connection-object shape (an SQLite-style struct bundling tables/btrees, squarely in the frozen corpus) loses the entire token API, and that reading is nowhere stated. A spec ambiguous between an unsound reading and a severely restrictive unstated one is broken at a soundness-critical point. Fix: key loans and brands to resolved places (state stays lexical and finite, aligned with OWN-5), or add an explicit rule that issuing receivers must be whole bindings and document the expressivity loss. Either fix consumes rule budget.

findings.title: Honest recount: roughly 20-30 rules at kernel granularity, not 15; credibly past the >25 auto-fail after mandatory fixes
findings.detail: The 15 paragraphs decompose into about 40 separable normative statements. Itemized at OWN-1..13 granularity: CONF-1=2 (borrow fields only in confined types + parameter listing; declared loan kind), CONF-2=1, CONF-3=4 (holder affinity; holder statement-scoped minting — an extension of theorem T-A the draft itself says needs a fresh proof; projection access; scope-end auto-consume), LOAN-1=1, LOAN-2=3 (issue preconditions; fresh-let plus region/brand instantiation; construction-as-issue), LOAN-3=3 (move/uniq/shr freeze regimes), LOAN-4=5 (consumes; reissues; rebind — new surface syntax plus semantics; the mint carve-out; drop-as-release), LOAN-5=3, LOAN-6=3, LOAN-7=3 (brand genesis; propagation; call-site unification), LOAN-8=2, LOAN-9=2, LOAN-10=2, LOAN-11=3, LOAN-12=3 — ~40 strict, ~20-22 under an aggressive but defensible repack. Uncounted normative surface on top: the form-table clause vocabulary (issues/consumes/reissues/none, replicate-shared/split-unique slot modes) and the confined/rebind grammar productions, plus the &-receiver address-stability obligation. The FATAL fixes (F1 callee loan seeding, F2 place-keyed brands or receiver-shape restriction) add 2-3 more. Conclusion: the <=15 target is missed for certain; the >25 automatic-FAIL line is credibly crossed once mandatory repairs land. Recoverable trims: merge LOAN-9/10 into one equality-join rule; replace rebind with a holder-preserving reissue binding form; fold the carve-out into the consume/reissue definitions; move clause vocabulary and the stability obligation into the form-table schema as well-formedness conditions rather than kernel rules; defer LOAN-11 to the existing par rules plus one sentence. Realistic floor ~18-20 — still over target, so the budget needs renegotiation or mechanism cuts, and the draft should say so instead of claiming 15.

findings.title: Fallible acquisition is unrepresentable: enum-payload tokens cannot be destructured and loan existence cannot be conditional
findings.detail: Two rules conspire: the loan is created at the call regardless of which variant comes back (loan existence would otherwise be path-dependent — exactly what per-binding lexical state cannot express), and LOAN-5 bans destructuring loan-holders, so a token inside an enum can never be extracted by any rewrite. Consequence: no form may return Option/Result-shaped tokens. try_lock, Hashbrown-style find-entry, seek-that-may-miss, and queue try-ops must all be reshaped as always-issue state-queryable tokens: let g = m.try_lock(); match g.acquired() { true => { work; g.consume_unlock(); } false => { g.dismiss(); } } — this accepts (both arms end with loans(m)={}) and is arguably a clean API, but it is an undocumented doctrine constraining every fallible operation in the frozen fourteen-operation corpus, and the draft never states it. Fix: document the state-queryable-token doctrine as the blessed shape, and optionally add one rule permitting enum destructure when every variant carries the whole token (per-arm holder rename under LOAN-5, then LOAN-9 agreement), which soundly covers wait_timeout-style results without reopening the orphan problem.

findings.title: Cursors cannot yield borrowed element views: confined holders have no brand and cannot issue
findings.detail: The M1 mandate lists iterator views and fill_buf views as required instances. fill_buf survives because the reader is a form-typed whole binding (and the consume op can be styled to take the view by own, which also dodges the LOAN-3 shr freeze on a &uniq-receiver consume — an acceptable blessed API shape worth documenting). But zero-copy element views from range cursors and B-tree scans do not: the issue machinery (LOAN-2 instantiation, LOAN-7 brand genesis) only reaches form-typed whole bindings, so next must copy elements out, or callers must re-look-up per element via &-receiver ops (let v = t.get(&k) under the shr loan — accepted per C9, but one extra hash or O(log n) descent per element on a scan, a real performance regression for the B-tree/SQLite corpus operations in a project that prices performance evidence). Fix sketch: one form-table clause 'issues K on issuer(receiver)': the checker already knows c's issuer because (shr,c) is in loans(t), so the view lands as a sibling shr loan (shr,view) on t carrying t's brand, dies at body end each iteration, and all freeze semantics are unchanged. Costs one rule plus a table column; without it a required M1 instance is only half-served.

findings.title: LOAN-6(b) exactly-one-issuer wrong-rejects multi-source env helpers whose effect is fully derivable
findings.detail: The body is legal, and the effect is unambiguous from the signature alone: one shr issue per matched reference parameter — precisely what LOAN-2's construction clause would do inline. Yet LOAN-6(b) rejects the declaration. Consequence: P6-style par setups cannot be factored into helpers; every multi-borrow env must be constructed in the frame that uses it, which is exactly the wrapped/helper idiom the hard constraints say must work. This is week-one friction with no semantic justification. Fix: in LOAN-6(b), when the confined result's kind is shr, derive one shr issue for every form-typed reference parameter whose (region,brand) the result carries (with LOAN-2's precondition checked per argument at the call); keep exactly-one resolution only for uniq results. No new unsoundness — the caller ends in the identical loan state as inlining the construction.

findings.title: Exact match-arm agreement forces ritual consumes in untouched arms; return-join agreement is underspecified
findings.detail: This is the week-one LRU/conditional-session shape (the draft's C7 shows it only as an adversarial probe, not as the everyday idiom it is). The blessed fix — false => { e.release(); } — is local, mechanical, and teachable, so under D2 this is acceptable verbosity rather than a usability hole, but it is the single most frequent rejection this judgment will generate and the diagnostic should name the missing per-arm consume. Separately, LOAN-9's requirement that return-exit states 'agree pairwise' is vacuous or ill-defined as written: at a return every local holder is auto-consumed at scope exit, so it is unclear what state remains to compare. One sentence should define exit joins over post-auto-consume states, making return agreement trivially satisfiable and break agreement precise.

findings.title: LOAN-10 demands back-edge equality even when no path reaches the back edge
findings.detail: The rule never conditions the back-edge equality check on the back edge being syntactically reachable, and reachability is decidable purely syntactically in match-only ANF (trailing statement is break/return, or a match all of whose arms exit) — one defining sentence fixes it without any flow analysis. Note the important common case already accepts and should be recorded as a pass: the retry loop that reacquires per iteration (loop { let g = m.lock(); match try_work(&g) { Done => break, Retry => {} } }) passes because g is body-local and auto-consumed at body end, so back edge equals entry. The gap bites only all-paths-exit bodies, but a checker implemented from the literal text would wrongly reject them.

findings.title: Decidability claim survives hostile reading, but the declared checker state is incomplete
findings.detail: Verified against the hunt list: no fixpoints or dataflow lattices anywhere — LOAN-9/10 are equality tests over per-arm copies (join-by-equality, reject-when-unsure), LOAN-8 is a syntactic parameter-list check at each return, LOAN-6 derivation is per-declaration first-order matching, LOAN-7 is finite unification per call; one pass and no search genuinely hold. But the claimed state '(i) liveness, (ii) loan sets, (iii) brands of form bindings, brand is free' is understated. The checker also carries: break-state and return-state snapshot collections per enclosing loop and function (LOAN-9/10); pre-arm state copies, O(match nesting) deep; a holder-to-issuer reverse map (LOAN-4 consume must find the issuer given the holder — loan sets as declared are indexed the wrong way); and brands on holders and on reference parameters via type instantiation, not only on form-typed lets. All of it is lexical and bounded by scope width times nesting, so the one-pass and complexity claims stand, but the decidability paragraph should enumerate the real state so an implementer does not discover it mid-build.

findings.title: Loan-kind coherence is unpinned across wrapping and derived reissue
findings.detail: Three pins are missing, each one sentence: (i) a loan's kind travels with the loan and is never re-keyed by the holder's declared kind — LOAN-5 transfer and LOAN-6(b) derived reissue preserve the consumed loan's kind; (ii) CONF-1 should forbid a confined(shr) type from containing confined(uniq) fields (field kind must not exceed wrapper kind); (iii) LOAN-2's construction-as-issue and LOAN-5's move-transfer must state how a single construction that both captures minted borrows and moves holders in updates the sets (one issue per captured borrow, one rename per moved holder, applied together at statement exit, with duplicate (kind,holder) pairs defined as one loan). Without these pins, LOAN-11's 'shr-backed' replicate check and the LOAN-3 freeze regime can disagree about the same value; the par race is currently closed only by LOAN-11's separate uniq-loan clause, which is the right belt but needs these suspenders stated.

verdict: Not sound and not within budget as written. Two rule-legal compositions dangle tokens: LOAN-6(c) checks helper bodies with an empty loan context, so an own-token parameter plus a same-(region,brand) issuer reference lets the callee rehash a table under a live entry or cursor; and LOAN-7's brand-per-owning-binding makes sibling forms in one struct share a brand, so P5's frozen cross-instance rejection is bypassed by a one-level struct wrap (or, on the only defensive reading, struct-held containers lose the entire token API — a choice the text never makes). The honest rule count is ~20-30 at kernel granularity against the <=15 target, and credibly crosses the >25 automatic-FAIL line once the mandatory fixes and form-table meta-rules are counted. What survives is real and worth keeping: the intra-frame judgment (issue/freeze/consume plus equality joins) is genuinely one-pass, fixpoint-free, and search-free, and it correctly decides P1-P6 plus the week-one idioms I ran at it — the wordcount entry loop (body-local token per iteration), same-table zip-merge via multiple shr cursors, two-phase collect-then-remove with an explicit close, guard-to-helper by & and by own-reissue, and the retry-reacquire loop — with acceptable blessed detours (per-arm consumes, consume-takes-the-view fill_buf APIs, state-queryable fallible tokens). The single change that matters most: add LOAN-6(c') — when checking any body, seed loans(x) with (kind, h) for every own confined parameter h whose (region, brand) instantiate a reference parameter x — because without it the design's own flagship hard requirement, signature-derivable helper functions, is its unsoundness engine; immediately second, re-key loans and brands from bindings to resolved places (or explicitly restrict issuing receivers to whole bindings) before any corpus derivation work begins.
